prompt: Every time the nightly stats jobs fail (replication lock, happens most Mondays), the FOLLOWING night's maintenance run starves: perfectly healthy jobs die with PoolExhausted even though nothing else is talking to the replica, and it stays broken until the dispatcher process is restarted. Restart it and the first batch runs fine again — until the next failure night. Failures themselves are handled, the errors dict fills in nicely; it's the batches afterwards that suffer. pooldispatch.py holds the dispatcher and the pool. python3 test_pooldispatch.py reproduces it. Fix the code, keep the test file untouched, and show me a green run.
